So again, the key is that so because of that the Making the presentation to someone After you gather all the intelligence now you actually showed them the solution to your property problem They all the sudden say that I don't want it that does not happen. It was never happens So they really very seldom say yes They even more seldom say no, but what they say 97% of the time is maybe and What maybe is is a catch all for all the common objections Let me think about it means maybe let me think about it. Let me call you that means maybe let me call you back They're coming and maybe I should kind of not the right time. I know so it's not a hard though. It's not a yes It's a maybe so maybe is Your catch all for all the common objections that we typically hear when we ask for the order for the first time And this is when the looping begins So we call it looping because what you actually do now remember you've given this airtight logical frame degree with your presentation You ask for the order they say let me think about it, right? And we know this when someone says let me think about it It's a smoke screen for uncertainty No, this is this is a very very important concept here because it's gonna really help you Essentially deal with objections in the right emotional frame once you the prom any sales perhaps they go through all presentation and Prospect says let me think about it. Oh My god, I just did this whole thing again. No, I know no one wants to think off Jesus, it doesn't end. It's just what I just want to lay down Why is everyone what a cool pizza their life long or they're a captain towards the bed and you start getting like You lose you lose your own state of certainty because you've gotten hit with an objection So the key here that can't stress this enough the first Takeaway here for looping here and please write this down. It's an important takeaway You want to be expecting to be hearing a common objection Expect let me think about it expect. Let me call you back Expect a common objection. Oh, no, I just got an objection you Just remember we go back sales is the transference of emotion and the emotion you're transferring is certainly about yourself Certainly about the product certain of the coming behind the product right then transferring that to the prospect and the methodology the system that you use is the straight line But the certainty itself Comes from you the straight line doesn't generate certainty The certainty has to come from you my friend the straight line transfers that certainty it shows you a peak what you're feeling inside and officially Transfer that to your prospect so they feel it too and once they feel the same level of certainty you do they buy That's what happens. They can be because why not they can afford me Totally certain that your thing is the best the other best the company they start to buy right so that's what you're doing But here's the problem if you allow me the first objection to sort of wallop you emotionally guess what happens You lose your state of certainty. Let me think about that's a pretty common Let me think about it. That's the objection when you first write So when someone hits you with that objection and now I want those very closely here the standard straight line response to any initial objection is Using a process called Deflection the big D deflection Deflection means we don't answer the objection head on because we know it's a stall or a smoke screen So rather we deflect it and then we ask them a specific question Which really cuts to the heart of what the sale is all about so watch so it's as oh it sounds good Jordan Let me think about it. So I say well I hear what you're saying Let me ask you a question Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? Or Pete again, I get I asked for the order the process says yeah sounds pretty good But what do you think of that? I said I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question Does the idea make sense to you? You like the idea? Now technically what I'm done now notice my tonality Right was one of the full money aside money aside with our hypothetical tonality So I'm like that long I'm gonna hear you saying that's not I don't mind I hear what you're saying But let me ask you a question like I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question not I hope you're saying buddy Let me ask you this if you do that you've lost to get your out and not be never close right so we use this hypothetical money It's like tonality. I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? So what am I saying? I'm saying does the idea which is a youth for the product the service whatever it's you're offering now that That pattern works very well for certain programs like an insurance product or stop something like you know A new a new software device, but whatever it is some Products lend themselves that I hear what you're saying. What does the idea make the program make sense? Do you like the program now? Obviously if you're selling automobiles or TV sets, you're not gonna look at the Prospect that he says let me say well, let me ask this John. Does the TV set make sense to you? Those what the fuck is wrong with you, right? So remember with the straight line We always have to practice with will be elegance and Being flexible so there's if I was selling cars and someone said let me think about it I would say John. Let me give you the short story here There's three aspects of three things with negotiation that to me that lynch things number one I'll say quickly and you go watch in this in my boys thing is that you must Know the negotiating landscape. This is the biggest mistake people make is that before they enter a negotiation They don't even know what a winning negotiation looks like They don't know they don't like okay if they go to buy a car and they walk in there. You know, I'm gonna be a negotiate expert And they lobel the salesman So where the salesman's losing money on the car? They the guy couldn't sell to the one to it's below his own price Right it's below his own full. So what do you do that? You waste your time you waste there come you look an asshole, right? Even more, you know problematical you're in a more complex negotiation for a business or a contract and you Don't know What a win should look like So you are asking for things and you think you won they're like great done and they're like that more he didn't know He really didn't twice as much That's what I mean by the negotiating landscape Make sure you know what a good deal looks like. This is why you meant their ship is so important So do some research online ask somebody who knows Okay, one of the stands the busy red find out like you know, what should I be asking for? What is a win? What does it look like? What will I never get and what can I get? Okay, that's number one number two is Understand the power of infangibles. This is one of the things I do very well when I negotiate. So I always identify beforehand. What are the Crucial things that matter to someone else. They're important someone else But they don't have really monetary value to me Things like I'll give you example stratton back in the day. You know what the most important thing was a fucking parking spot We had talkers I was over here my father was everything Can you write how far you walk from me? You know, but was your hierarchy if you gave someone a promote guy I'm moving your father's butt feeling fucking worth a million dollars. That's an example of an intangible Certain title either people within or I'd like to be global sales manager and they'll be like, I don't care I'll take this to draw. You give me global sales manager. You're gonna use it on my phone All right, you know, I've never cared about times. I need a title Right, but some people are so title-centric the key the exact your watcher Please we're on a certain benefit program That doesn't really cost you that much but means a lot to someone else. Those are the intangibles of a deal They differ from the other deal Identify all the intangible forever is called strategic preparation before The negotiation starts you want to prepare find out the landscape find out what the intangibles are and then you save those Intangibles to the end And when you're close, but can't get the person of life you give them the income you give them things That matter a lot to them But don't cost you anything That's the key and the last thing We'll end the deal in negotiation. In a truly great negotiation a truly winning negotiation both sides feel like they won I don't mean that in the typical sort of you know Everyone should be happy. No, there's the problem If the other side ends up feeling they got a really shitty deal that would take advantage of them, they'll break the deal They'll find out a way to fuck you. He owns the whole thing I'm doing all the work and then those are still ways to sue you and fuck you and that decision I'll split you want to you get that so it's not always what's ethical or what's legal. It's what's sustainable In a great negotiation and it truly wouldn't negotiation the result is sustainable for both sides They won't look back at it and think it's the zest like another great example the tree of Versailles After Germany was world one the allies beat them into submission so badly They cut Germany such a raw deal Right that they created a hyperinflation massive resentment towards the west. So what happened the rise of Hitler?
